page.title: Help:Introduction to referencing with Wiki Markup/2

revision.comment: reword parts that were redundant/no clear enough; link where appropriate and makes most sense since this isn't an article all professional also, it must be approachable/soft/but knowledgeable. also remove the link that is _not_ principle of least surprise
revision.text:
<noinclude>{{pp-semi|small=yes}}
</noinclude>{{intro to|
{{Shortcut|WP:INTREF2}}

[[Wikipedia:Inline citations|Inline citations]] are usually small, numbered footnotes like this.<ref>{{cite book |author-last=Wales |author-first=Jimmy |title=What is an inline citation? |date={{CURRENTYEAR}} |publisher=Wikipublisher |page=6}}</ref> They are generally added either immediately following the fact that they support, or at the end of the sentence that they support. When clicked, they take the reader to a [[Wikipedia:Citing sources|citation]] in a reference section near the bottom of the article.

While editing a page that uses the most common footnote style, you will see inline citations displayed between <syntaxhighlight inline lang=wikitext><ref>...</ref></syntaxhighlight> tags.

If you are creating a new page, or adding references to a page that didn't previously have any, remember to add a [[Wikipedia:Reference section|references section]] like the one below [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout#Order of article elements|near the end]] of the article:
<syntaxhighlight lang=wikitext>
==References==
{{reflist}}
</syntaxhighlight>

{{a note|}}Note that this is by far the most popular way to use [[Wikipedia:Inline citations|inline citations]], but sometimes you will find other styles being used in an article. This [[WP:CITEVAR|is acceptable]], and you shouldn't change it or mix styles. To add a new reference, just copy and modify an existing one.

{{blockquote|
{{fake heading|sub=2|References}}
{{reflist}}
----
}}
}}

<noinclude>{{pp-semi|small=yes}}
</noinclude>{{intro to|
{{Shortcut|WP:INTREF2}}

[[Wikipedia:Inline citations|Inline citations]] are usually small, numbered footnotes like this.<ref>{{cite book |author-last=Wales |author-first=Jimmy |title=What is an inline citation? |date={{CURRENTYEAR}} |publisher=Wikipublisher |page=6}}</ref> They are generally added either immediately following the fact that they support, or at the end of the sentence that they support. When clicked, they take the reader to a [[Wikipedia:Citing sources|citation]] in a reference section near the bottom of the article.

While editing a page that uses the most common footnote style, you will see inline citations displayed between <syntaxhighlight inline lang=wikitext><ref>...</ref></syntaxhighlight> tags.

If you are creating a new page, or adding references to a page that didn't previously have any, remember to add a [[Wikipedia:Reference section|references section]] like the one below [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout#Order of article elements|near the end]] of the article:
<syntaxhighlight lang=wikitext>
==References==
{{reflist}}
</syntaxhighlight>

{{a note|}}Note that this is by far the most popular way to use [[Wikipedia:Inline citations|inline citations]], but sometimes you will find other styles being used in an article. This [[WP:CITEVAR|is acceptable]], and you shouldn't change it or mix styles. To add a new reference, just copy and modify an existing one.

{{blockquote|
{{fake heading|sub=2|References}}
{{reflist}}
----
}}
}}
